Strategic decisions backed by direct execution

The work behind Tam Tam Corp is informed by long-term, hands-on experience with advertising, digital systems, and operational tooling — long before the company itself existed. Focus areas include service businesses, e-commerce, real estate, and professional services requiring reliable automation with direct accountability.

Built by the person you work with

Systems are designed and implemented personally by the founder, not delegated to a team you never meet.

Decisions come from execution knowledge — the person consulting is the same person who has built, deployed, and maintained similar systems in production.

This matters when things get complex. No game of telephone. No layers between the problem and the solution. One accountable point of responsibility from idea to execution.

Human + AI Collaboration
AI executes. Humans decide.

AI.bo is Tam Tam Corp's internal AI assistant — a speed and scale multiplier, not a replacement for human responsibility.

It handles execution: drafting, structuring, processing, analyzing. But final decisions, system design, and client accountability remain with the founder. All AI outputs are reviewed, tested, and validated by a human before deployment.

This is how we scale without losing responsibility. Decisions and implementation remain human-led.

When to Choose Tam Tam Corp

Best suited for companies that need implementation with direct accountability, not layered agencies or advisory-only consulting

Good fit
Founders & SMEs needing hands-on implementation
Operations-heavy businesses that need reliable systems
Companies requiring direct accountability from design to deployment
Businesses seeking long-term system partners with technical depth
Not a fit
Projects requiring large development teams or agency scale
Low-budget, high-volume production work
"Set it and forget it" buyers expecting zero maintenance
Clients seeking advisory-only consulting without implementation
How We Work

Structured, predictable, and grounded in execution

01
Understand the real problem

We dig into what's actually slowing you down, not what you think the solution should be.

02
Design the system

We map out automation logic, integrations, and workflows that fit your operations.

03
Build & integrate

We implement the system, connect it to your existing tools, and ensure it runs reliably.

04
Iterate and maintain

We refine based on real usage and provide ongoing support as your needs evolve.

Example System (Anonymized)
Example: Internal Automation System for a Service-Based Business

A service-based company was handling inquiries, internal handovers, and follow-ups manually across multiple tools. This resulted in delays, duplicated work, and unclear responsibility.

Tam Tam Corp designed and implemented an internal automation system that connected inbound communication, task routing, and status tracking into a single workflow. AI was used to handle classification, routing, and availability, while approvals and decisions remained human-led.

The system reduced manual coordination, improved response consistency, and gave the business a clear operational overview. All components were built to be maintainable, transparent, and adaptable as processes evolved.

If you're looking for speed with responsibility, this is how we work.

Demo
AI Automation Consulting for Businesses That Need Reliable Execution

We design and implement automation systems that actually run in production — built personally, not delegated.

The Problem

Most businesses have the same problem: manual processes that should be automated but never are.

They waste hours on data entry, copy-paste between systems, manual report generation, and repetitive customer responses. Everyone knows this should be automated, but it never happens because:

Existing tools are too rigid for specific workflows
Custom development is expensive and slow
Off-the-shelf AI solutions promise everything but integrate poorly
Internal teams lack the time or technical depth to build automation

The result is operational friction that compounds daily. Systems that could talk to each other do not. Tasks that could run automatically still require human intervention.

What We Do

We design and build AI automation systems tailored to your actual operations, not theoretical use cases.

Typical implementations include:

Automated data processing between disconnected systems
AI-powered document analysis and information extraction
Workflow orchestration that replaces manual handoffs
Intelligent routing and classification of incoming requests
Custom reporting that pulls from multiple sources automatically

These are production systems. They run daily, handle edge cases, and integrate with your existing infrastructure without requiring constant maintenance.

How This Is Built

The founder designs and implements these systems directly. There is no handover to junior developers or anonymous teams. Every line of system logic is reviewed and validated by the same person who consults on the strategy.

This matters because automation projects fail when decisions are made by people who do not understand your operations or have not built similar systems before. When the person consulting is also the person implementing, the feedback loop is immediate and decisions are grounded in execution knowledge developed from maintaining production systems.

Why this approach works: Most automation projects fail because consultants design systems they cannot build, or developers build systems they do not understand strategically. We avoid this by concentrating both capabilities in one accountable person. Common mistakes like over-engineering, poor error handling, or brittle integrations are prevented through direct execution experience.

Systems are built iteratively. We start with the most painful manual process, automate it, ensure it runs reliably in production, then move to the next. This reduces risk and delivers value incrementally rather than promising everything upfront and delivering nothing that works.

Human Judgment. AI Precision.

AI.bo is Tam Tam Corp's internal AI assistant. It accelerates execution by handling repetitive tasks, drafting code, and processing large datasets.

But system architecture, business logic decisions, and client responsibility remain human. AI increases speed and scale. It does not replace accountability.

Who This Is For
Good fit
Founders who need systems, not consultants who just advise
SMEs with operations-heavy workflows
Businesses that have tried automation before and been burned
Teams that value accountability over scale
Not a fit
Businesses looking for cheap automation providers
Expectations of one-click AI solving everything
Projects requiring large development teams
Clients who want automation but not involvement

If this problem sounds familiar, let's talk.

Discuss Your System

AI-powered business process automation for modern companies.

AIProductImagery:
Back to Home
AI Product Imagery — Founder-Led Implementation

E-commerce businesses often need consistent product imagery at scale without photoshoot logistics. At Tam Tam Corp, we implement AI-assisted image generation systems for catalog and marketing use — custom workflows, not template tools. Humans define brand standards and approve final outputs.

What This Service Actually Is

This is not an "AI image generator subscription." It represents a tailored image production workflow designed for your product catalog, brand guidelines, and output requirements.

Our founder-led approach means one person is responsible for understanding your visual standards, implementing generation rules, and ensuring consistency — no delegation to teams unfamiliar with your brand.

Human + AI Collaboration: AI generates image variations based on defined parameters. Humans set brand standards, approve outputs, and make final decisions on what represents the brand publicly.

How This Works
Brand Standards Definition

You define visual style, background preferences, lighting rules. AI generates within these constraints.

Batch Processing

AI processes product images in batches according to catalog requirements and output specs.

Human Review & Approval

Final outputs require human review before publishing. Quality control remains with your team.

Catalog Integration

Approved images sync with your e-commerce platform or asset management system.

Typical Situation (Without Guaranteed Outcomes)
E-commerce with Large SKU Count

Businesses with hundreds of products struggle to maintain consistent imagery across catalogs. Traditional photoshoots are expensive and slow. Product updates require re-shooting.

We implement an AI-assisted workflow that generates product images in consistent environments. Brand guidelines are encoded as generation rules. Your team reviews and approves outputs before they go live. The system handles volume; humans ensure brand integrity.

Engagement Process (Consultative + Implementation)
1
Visual Standards Documentation

We document your brand guidelines, product photography style, and output requirements. Humans define what "acceptable" looks like.

2
Generation Workflow Setup

Build processing pipeline with brand constraints encoded. Test with sample products to ensure style consistency.

3
Review & Approval Process

Establish human review checkpoints. Define who approves outputs before they go live.

4
Catalog Integration & Monitoring

Connect to your e-commerce platform. Monitor outputs for consistency drift. Adjust rules as brand evolves.

Who This Is For / Who This Is Not For
✓ This is for you if:
• You have clear brand guidelines and visual standards
• Your catalog has significant SKU volume requiring consistent imagery
• You want founder-led implementation with brand accountability
• You understand that humans must approve final outputs
✗ This is NOT for you if:
• You want a self-service image generation tool
• You expect AI to maintain brand standards autonomously
• You need immediate outputs without workflow design
• You prefer SaaS platforms over custom workflows
Investment

This service is delivered as a tailored consulting engagement. We do not offer self-service tools. Implementation is deliberate, scoped, and aligned with brand accountability.

Scope-Dependent

Pricing determined after brand standards and volume assessment

• Includes: Brand standards documentation, generation workflow, review process, catalog integration

• Implementation is founder-led with clear accountability

• Systems are built for operational ownership, not vendor dependency

Engagement Expectations

Conversations are scheduled only after understanding your catalog requirements and brand standards clearly. Not every inquiry leads to implementation. We work with businesses that value founder-led execution and human oversight.

About Tam Tam Corp

Founder-led. Detail-driven. Built from the inside.

Founder-led. Detail-driven. Built from the inside.

Tam Tam Corp FZE LLC is a founder-led consultancy built and operated by Ibrahim Surucu, focusing on AI automation, systems design, and hands-on implementation. Systems are built personally by the founder, not delegated to teams. This means one identifiable human remains responsible for system design, decisions, and implementation from strategy to deployment, maintaining consistency between what is recommended and what is delivered.

From idea to implementation.

Systems are designed to solve operational problems, not to showcase technology. The focus is on bridging business requirements with technical execution—building tools that teams actually use in daily operations. No theoretical solutions. Only implementations tested and maintained in production environments.

THIS IS HOW WE SCALE WITHOUT LOSING RESPONSIBILITY.

Human judgment. AI precision.

AI is used to accelerate execution, not replace human judgment. AI.bo handles drafting, structuring, and processing tasks. But all strategic decisions, system design, and client accountability remain with the founder. Every AI output is reviewed and validated before deployment. Decisions and implementation remain human-led.

What this means for clients
Direct communication

No layers. No forwarding. You work with the person who understands and builds your system.

Clear responsibility

One accountable mind from the first idea to the final delivery. No excuses, no gaps.

Faster execution

Decisions happen quickly because there are no internal approvals or handovers to slow things down.

Systems are built for long-term use, not demonstrations. Tam Tam Corp does not operate as a high-volume agency and does not resell generic or prebuilt AI solutions. Tam Tam Corp operates in the UAE and globally, serving SMEs and operations-heavy businesses that require clear accountability from design to deployment.
BusinessAutomation:
Back to Home
Business Process Automation — Founder-Led Implementation

Operations-heavy businesses often have repetitive processes that resist standardization. At Tam Tam Corp, we design automation systems for document processing, email workflows, and multi-step orchestration — custom logic, not templated solutions. AI executes within rules. Humans define exceptions and approvals.

What This Service Actually Is

This is not a "workflow automation platform." It represents a customized operational system designed to handle your specific process complexity, approval hierarchies, and exception scenarios.

Our founder-led approach means one person is accountable for understanding your operational logic, mapping dependencies, and ensuring automation supports human oversight — not replacing judgment.

Human + AI Collaboration: AI automates predictable execution within defined parameters. Humans set business rules, approve exceptions, and maintain operational responsibility.

How This Works
Process Mapping

Document current workflows, decision points, and exception handling. Humans define what can be automated.

Rule-Based Execution

AI handles routine tasks — data extraction, routing, notifications — within established rules.

Human Approval Points

Exceptions and non-routine scenarios escalate to designated humans for decision.

System Integration

Connects existing tools — CRM, email, storage — without platform replacement.

Typical Situation (Without Guaranteed Outcomes)
Operations Teams with Manual Data Entry

Many businesses process invoices, forms, or documents manually. Staff extract data, enter into systems, route for approval. Errors compound. Bottlenecks form at approval steps.

We design an automation system that extracts structured data from documents, validates against business rules, and routes to appropriate approvers. Humans define what triggers exceptions. AI handles predictable extraction and routing. Approval authority remains with operations leadership.

Engagement Process (Consultative + Implementation)
1
Operational Audit

We document your current process, identify repetitive tasks, and map decision points. Humans define what requires judgment.

2
Automation Design

Build execution logic that handles routine scenarios. Define exception triggers and escalation paths.

3
System Integration & Testing

Connect to existing tools. Test with real operational scenarios. Refine rules based on team feedback.

4
Deployment & Monitoring

Deploy live with your operations team. Monitor for edge cases. Adjust automation boundaries as processes evolve.

Who This Is For / Who This Is Not For
✓ This is for you if:
• Your team spends significant time on repetitive operational tasks
• You have clear business rules that can be codified
• You want founder-led implementation with operational accountability
• You understand that humans must handle exceptions
✗ This is NOT for you if:
• You want a plug-and-play automation platform
• You expect AI to handle all edge cases autonomously
• You need immediate deployment without process analysis
• You prefer pre-built workflow tools over custom logic
Investment

This service is delivered as a tailored consulting engagement. We do not offer templated automation. Implementation is deliberate, scoped, and aligned with operational responsibility.

Scope-Dependent

Pricing determined after operational audit and complexity assessment

• Includes: Process mapping, automation design, system integration, team training

• Implementation is founder-led with clear accountability

• Systems are built for operational ownership, not vendor lock-in

Engagement Expectations

Conversations are scheduled only after understanding your operational processes clearly. Not every inquiry leads to implementation. We work with businesses that value founder-led execution and human accountability.

Data & Security

Tam Tam Corp FZE LLC

Last updated: 30 December 2025

At Tam Tam Corp FZE LLC, data security and responsible system design are core principles — not afterthoughts. We design automation and AI systems with clear boundaries, human oversight, and security-first thinking.

1. Our Approach to Data

We collect and process only the data required to:

Deliver our services
Operate systems reliably
Communicate with clients

We do not:

Sell personal or business data
Trade data with third parties
Use data for advertising networks

Data is used strictly for operational and service-related purposes.

2. Human Responsibility & AI Systems

AI systems are powerful execution tools — but they do not replace human responsibility.

At Tam Tam Corp:

AI operates within predefined rules
Automation handles execution and repetition
Strategic decisions, approvals, and accountability remain human-led

AI systems do not make autonomous business decisions on behalf of clients.

3. Data Processing & Storage

Depending on the project, data may be processed using:

Secure cloud infrastructure
Communication platforms (e.g. messaging tools)
Analytics or monitoring systems

Third-party tools are used only as data processors and are selected based on:

Security standards
Reliability
Relevance to the task

Data is stored only for as long as necessary to fulfill its purpose.

4. Client Data Confidentiality

All non-public client information is treated as confidential.

This includes:

Business data
Operational workflows
Technical configurations
Internal documentation

Access is limited to what is necessary for system design, implementation, and maintenance.

5. Security Measures

We apply reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect data, including:

Access controls
Environment separation
Secure authentication where applicable
Responsible handling of credentials and API keys

While no system can be guaranteed to be completely risk-free, security is treated as a continuous responsibility.

6. WhatsApp & Communication Security

When systems involve WhatsApp or similar platforms:

Communication data is processed only to enable the service
Conversations are not reused for marketing
No data is resold or shared outside the intended scope

Platform-specific policies and limitations may apply.

7. Client Responsibilities

Clients are responsible for:

Providing data they are authorized to share
Complying with applicable data protection laws
Defining internal approval processes where required

Tam Tam Corp designs systems to support compliance but does not replace legal or regulatory obligations.

8. Incident Handling

In the event of a security or data-related incident:

We act promptly to assess and contain impact
Affected parties are informed where appropriate
Corrective measures are implemented to prevent recurrence
9. Transparency

We believe trust is built through clarity.

If you have questions about:

How data is handled
How AI systems operate
How security is managed

You can contact us at any time.

10. Contact

Tam Tam Corp FZE LLC

Founder-Led Consulting & Implementation for Businesses That Need Reliable Execution

We provide founder-led consulting and hands-on implementation for AI automation and systems — no layers, no delegation, one accountable mind from idea to execution.

The Problem

Most consulting firms sell advice but do not implement. Development agencies implement but do not understand the business problem. The result is a gap: consultants who cannot execute and developers who do not understand strategy.

This creates predictable failures:

Consultants produce PowerPoint decks that never become real systems
Agencies build what was specified, not what was actually needed
Projects get handed off between teams who do not share context
Feedback loops are slow because decision-makers are not implementers
Accountability is diffused across multiple people and no one owns outcomes

Businesses need someone who understands the problem, designs the solution, and implements it directly. Not a team of specialists. One person who sees the full picture and has the technical depth to execute.

What We Do

We provide consulting that includes implementation. The founder analyzes the problem, designs the system, and builds it personally. No handovers. No layers.

This means:

Strategic guidance backed by execution knowledge
System design that is technically feasible because it will be implemented by the same person
Fast feedback loops — adjustments happen immediately, not after committee discussions
Accountability concentrated in one person who owns both strategy and execution
No game of telephone between consultants, architects, and developers

This model works when problems are complex but scope is contained. It fails when projects require large teams or deep specialization in multiple domains. It succeeds when speed, accountability, and execution quality matter more than team size.

How This Is Built

The founder is directly involved from first conversation to production deployment. There are no junior team members building in the background. No anonymous developers you never meet. Every system component is reviewed, tested, and validated by the same person who designed the strategy.

Why this approach works: Systems fail when built by people who did not participate in the decision-making process. Requirements get lost in translation. Context disappears across handovers. Edge cases are missed because the implementer did not understand the full operational picture or business constraints. We avoid this through concentrated responsibility — one person owns both strategy and execution.

This matters because most consulting firms sell advice but cannot implement, while development agencies implement but do not understand business strategy. The gap between what is recommended and what is actually built creates predictable failures. We eliminate this gap through direct accountability.

When the person consulting is the person implementing, the feedback loop is instantaneous. Decisions are grounded in what can actually be built and maintained. Trade-offs are made with full awareness of implementation complexity. Adjustments happen in hours based on production reality, not weeks waiting for committee approvals.

Human Judgment. AI Precision.

AI.bo is Tam Tam Corp's internal AI assistant. It accelerates execution by handling repetitive work, drafting code, processing data, and maintaining documentation.

But strategic decisions, system architecture, and client accountability remain human. AI makes one person as productive as a small team. It does not replace the need for deep understanding and personal responsibility.

Who This Is For
Good fit
Founders who want execution, not just advice
Businesses burned by consulting firms that do not implement
Teams that value accountability over team size
Projects where speed and execution quality matter more than scale
Not a fit
Projects requiring large development teams
Businesses needing specialists in multiple technical domains
Clients who want consulting without implementation
Organizations that require committee-based decision-making

If this problem sounds familiar, let's talk.

Discuss Your System

AI-powered business process automation for modern companies.

HowWeDecide:
Back to Homepage
How We Decide

Automation and AI are not the hard part. Decisions are.

This page documents how Tam Tam Corp approaches responsibility, risk, and system design. Every implementation follows these principles.

Responsibility Comes First

Automation never removes accountability. Every system has a clear human owner. When something breaks or behaves unexpectedly, one person is responsible for the decision and the outcome.

Automation Follows Clarity

We do not automate unclear or unstable processes. Structure comes before speed. If a workflow cannot be documented clearly, it should not be automated yet.

AI Executes, Humans Decide

AI supports execution and availability. Decisions remain human-led. Every output from AI is validated before it impacts operations or reaches customers.

Failure Modes Are Designed Early

We consider what happens when systems fail before deploying them. Fallback paths, error handling, and recovery processes are not afterthoughts.

Simplicity Scales Better Than Complexity

We prefer systems that can be understood, maintained, and adjusted over time. A system that cannot be explained clearly to its users will eventually fail in production.

No System Without a Human Override

Every automated workflow includes a path back to human control. Automation improves efficiency, but humans must retain the ability to intervene when necessary.

If It Can't Be Explained, It's Not Ready

Systems deployed to clients must be understandable. If the logic behind a decision or recommendation cannot be explained in plain language, the system requires more work before deployment.

These principles guide how systems are designed, tested, and deployed. They exist to ensure reliability, maintain accountability, and prevent predictable failures.

LeadQualification:
Back to Home
Lead Qualification Systems — Founder-Led Implementation

Sales teams often struggle with distinguishing high-intent prospects from noise. At Tam Tam Corp, we design lead qualification systems that score and categorize inquiries based on your criteria — behavioral signals, budget indicators, decision authority. AI supports pattern recognition. Humans define what qualifies as "ready."

What This Service Actually Is

This is not a "lead scoring tool." It represents a custom qualification framework designed to filter inquiries based on your sales criteria — budget range, decision authority, implementation timeline, operational fit.

Our founder-led approach ensures that one accountable person defines your qualification logic, implements scoring rules, and integrates with your CRM — no delegation to a team that doesn't understand your business context.

Human + AI Collaboration: AI recognizes patterns in lead behavior and applies scoring rules. Humans define what "qualified" means, set thresholds for engagement, and make final decisions on high-value opportunities.

How This Works
Human-Defined Criteria

You define what qualifies a lead: budget, authority, need, timeline. AI scores based on these rules.

Behavioral Analysis

AI tracks engagement signals — email opens, site visits, content downloads — and updates scores.

CRM Integration

Scores sync with your existing CRM. Sales teams see prioritized leads without platform switching.

Human Oversight

Final engagement decisions remain with your sales team. AI informs, doesn't decide.

Typical Situation (Without Guaranteed Outcomes)
B2B Service Businesses with High Inquiry Volume

Many B2B companies receive dozens of inquiries weekly. Without qualification, sales teams waste time on unqualified prospects who lack budget, authority, or urgency.

We design a qualification system that scores leads based on company size, industry fit, expressed need, and engagement behavior. Sales teams focus on high-scoring opportunities. Lower-scoring leads enter nurture sequences. Criteria are defined by your sales leadership, not by AI assumptions.

Engagement Process (Consultative + Implementation)
1
Qualification Criteria Definition

We document your ideal customer profile, disqualification signals, and scoring thresholds. Humans set the rules.

2
Scoring Framework Design

Build a point-based system that weighs behavioral signals, firmographic data, and engagement patterns.

3
CRM Integration & Testing

Connect to your CRM, apply scoring rules to historical leads, refine thresholds based on actual conversion data.

4
Live Deployment & Refinement

Deploy live with your sales team. Monitor accuracy, adjust criteria based on feedback, ensure humans remain in control.

Who This Is For / Who This Is Not For
✓ This is for you if:
• Your sales team is overwhelmed with low-quality leads
• You have clear criteria for what makes a qualified prospect
• You want founder-led implementation, not vendor dependency
• You understand that AI scores, but humans decide
✗ This is NOT for you if:
• You want a plug-in scoring tool without customization
• You expect AI to autonomously decide who to engage
• You need instant results without criteria definition
• You prefer SaaS platforms over custom implementation
Investment

Delivered as a tailored consulting engagement

This service is not a subscription product. Implementation is deliberate, scoped, and aligned with your sales process.

Scope-Dependent

Pricing determined after qualification criteria assessment

• Includes: Criteria definition, scoring framework, CRM integration, sales team training

• Implementation is founder-led with direct accountability

• Systems are built for operational ownership, not vendor dependency

Engagement Expectations

Conversations are scheduled only after understanding your sales qualification criteria clearly. Not every inquiry leads to implementation. We work with businesses that value founder-led execution and human decision authority.

Frequently Asked Questions
What exactly is Persona Engine?

A controlled system that creates daily video content using your digital representation. One photo session. Daily presence. No filming required.

How does this differ from hiring a video team?

No scheduling. No filming days. No performance pressure. Content runs in the background while you focus on operations.

Do I need to review every video?

Approval checkpoints are built in. You define boundaries once. The system operates within them.

Campaign logic can be adjusted. Changes are implemented without disrupting daily output.

Yes. Controlled, consistent, and aligned with your positioning. Humans define strategy and boundaries.

System Architecture & Integrations for Businesses That Need Reliable Execution

We design and implement system integrations that connect disconnected tools into unified workflows — built personally, not delegated.

The Problem

Most businesses run on multiple disconnected systems. CRM, accounting, project management, customer support, and inventory systems all exist in silos. Data is manually copied between them because they do not talk to each other.

This creates operational friction that compounds daily:

Customer data exists in multiple places and is never fully synchronized
Manual data entry between systems introduces errors and delays
Reporting requires pulling information from different tools manually
Processes break when one tool is updated and integration stops working
Teams waste time on administrative work instead of productive tasks

Generic integration platforms like Zapier promise easy connections but fail on complex workflows or custom systems. Development agencies quote months of work for simple integrations. The result is stagnation: systems stay disconnected because integrating them properly feels impossible.

What We Do

We build integrations that make disconnected systems work as one unified platform. Data flows automatically. Processes run without manual intervention.

Typical implementations include:

Two-way synchronization between CRM, accounting, and project management tools
Automated data pipelines that move information between systems reliably
Custom API integrations for tools without native connections
Unified dashboards that pull data from multiple sources in real time
Workflow automation that triggers actions across different platforms
Data transformation and normalization between incompatible formats

These are not simple Zapier workflows. These are production integrations that handle edge cases, maintain data consistency, and continue working when underlying systems change.

How This Is Built

The founder designs and implements these integrations directly. System architecture is not delegated to teams that do not understand your full operational context. Every integration decision is validated against production requirements.

Why this approach works: Integration projects fail when built by people who only understand one side of the connection. They solve the happy path but break on edge cases because they do not see the full operational picture or understand how data flows through your business. We avoid this by maintaining end-to-end visibility — the person architecting the integration is the same person who debugs it when it breaks in production.

When the person consulting is the person implementing, decisions about data flow, error handling, and process logic are grounded in operational reality developed from maintaining similar integrations. Common mistakes like ignoring rate limits, poor error recovery, or data inconsistencies are prevented through production experience.

We build incrementally. Start with the most critical connection, ensure it runs reliably in production, monitor it under real load, then expand. This reduces risk and delivers value immediately rather than promising a unified system months from now that never materializes.

Human Judgment. AI Precision.

AI.bo accelerates integration development by analyzing API documentation, generating connection code, and handling data transformation logic.

But system architecture decisions — what connects to what, how data flows, what happens on errors — remain human. AI speeds execution. It does not replace the need for someone who understands both systems deeply and has built similar integrations before.

Who This Is For
Good fit
Businesses running multiple systems that do not talk to each other
Teams wasting hours on manual data entry between tools
Operations that break when information is out of sync
Businesses that have tried simple integration tools and hit limitations
Not a fit
Projects requiring enterprise-scale data warehousing
Systems with no APIs or accessible data layers
Businesses expecting instant integrations without proper planning
Complex legacy system migrations requiring large teams

If this problem sounds familiar, let's talk.

Discuss Your System

AI.bo — Assisted Communication & Intake Systems

Businesses face inquiry overload, availability issues, and slow response times. At Tam Tam Corp, we implement AI.bo — assisted intake, classification, and routing systems delivered through WhatsApp or other channels. AI handles execution within defined rules. Humans retain decision authority and accountability.

What This Actually Is

AI.bo is not a product. It is an internal execution layer implemented as part of consulting engagements — designed for intake, classification, availability management, and routing through WhatsApp or other channels.

Our founder-led approach ensures one accountable person understands your workflows, builds the system, and maintains it — no delegation to teams unfamiliar with your business.

Human + AI Collaboration: AI.bo handles conversational intake and classification. Humans define logic, set boundaries, approve outcomes, and retain decision-making authority. No autonomous operation.

How This Works
Human-Defined Logic

You define qualification criteria, approval points, and escalation rules. AI executes within these boundaries.

Conversational Intake

WhatsApp-based systems capture structured information through natural conversation flow.

Decision Points Preserved

AI handles predictable routing and responses. Humans intervene at critical decision moments.

Integration with Operations

Connected to your CRM, calendar, and internal tools — not a standalone product.

Typical Situations (Without Guaranteed Outcomes)
Service Businesses

Inbound inquiry handling with qualification logic

Businesses struggle with consistent follow-up. We implement AI.bo-assisted systems that codify qualification criteria and route inquiries to the right human at the right time.

Professional Services

Initial information gathering before consultations

Reduce time spent on unqualified prospects. System collects structured information before human engagement begins.

Multi-Location Operations

Location-based routing and appointment coordination

Customers reach the correct team member based on location, availability, and service type — defined by operational rules, not AI guesswork.

Engagement Process (Consultative + Implementation)
1
Workflow Mapping

We document your current inquiry process, qualification criteria, and decision points. This defines where AI assists and where humans remain accountable.

2
System Design

Custom conversational flows are built to your operational logic. No templates. Rules are explicit, not inferred by AI.

3
Implementation & Integration

AI.bo deployment via WhatsApp Business API, CRM integration, and internal tool connection. Founder-led implementation, not delegated to a team.

4
Human Testing & Refinement

Real-world testing with your team. Adjustments based on operational feedback, not theoretical optimization.

Who This Is For / Who This Is Not For
✓ This is for you if:
• You need structured inquiry handling with clear qualification rules
• Your team spends significant time on repetitive inbound questions
• You want founder-led implementation with direct accountability
• You understand that humans must define operational logic
✗ This is NOT for you if:
• You want a plug-and-play chatbot product
• You expect AI to make business decisions autonomously
• You need immediate deployment without workflow analysis
• You prefer layered agency teams over founder-led execution
Investment

This service is delivered as a tailored consulting engagement. We do not offer "plug-and-play" automation. Implementation is deliberate, scoped, and aligned with human decision responsibilities.

Engagement Expectations

WhatsApp Bots & Conversational Automation for Businesses That Need Reliable Execution

We design and implement WhatsApp automation systems that handle customer service, appointment booking, and lead qualification 24/7 — built personally, not delegated.

The Problem

Customers expect instant responses on WhatsApp, but businesses cannot staff 24/7 support or afford to miss inquiries outside office hours.

The result is lost leads, frustrated customers, and teams overwhelmed by repetitive questions. Manual WhatsApp management means:

Delayed responses lead to customers moving to competitors
Staff answering the same questions repeatedly
No tracking of conversations or automatic follow-up
Appointment booking requires back-and-forth messaging
Lead information is captured manually or not at all

Most chatbot solutions are either too simple to handle real conversations or too complex to integrate properly. They sound promising in demos but fail in production because they cannot handle edge cases or connect to actual business systems.

What We Do

We build WhatsApp bots that handle real customer interactions — not just scripted responses.

Typical implementations include:

24/7 customer service automation with AI understanding of intent
Appointment booking that checks availability and confirms automatically
Lead qualification that asks the right questions and scores responses
FAQ handling with natural language understanding
Automatic handover to humans when needed
Integration with CRM, calendar systems, and internal databases

These bots do not just reply. They extract information, update systems, trigger workflows, and maintain context across conversations. They work in production, not just in controlled demos.

How This Is Built

The founder builds these systems directly. Conversation logic, integrations, and edge case handling are designed and implemented by the same person, ensuring consistency between strategy and execution.

Why this approach works: WhatsApp bots fail when built by teams that do not understand your business operations. They handle the happy path but break on real customer interactions because designers do not see production errors and developers do not understand business context. We avoid this by maintaining both design and implementation responsibility in one person who monitors production usage.

When the person consulting is the person implementing, adjustments happen immediately based on actual usage patterns observed in production. Conversation flows are refined based on real customer interactions, not theoretical scenarios.

We start with the most common customer interactions, automate those reliably, then expand. The bot learns from real conversations and improves iteratively rather than being built once and left to struggle.

Human Judgment. AI Precision.

AI.bo powers the conversational intelligence behind WhatsApp bots. It handles natural language understanding, intent recognition, and response generation at scale.

But conversation design, escalation rules, and business logic are defined by humans. AI makes bots available 24/7. It does not replace the need for human oversight and continuous refinement.

Who This Is For
Good fit
Service businesses overwhelmed by customer inquiries
Appointment-based businesses losing leads to slow responses
E-commerce brands needing 24/7 support without hiring full teams
Businesses where WhatsApp is the primary customer channel
Not a fit
Businesses expecting zero human oversight
Projects requiring multi-language support for dozens of languages
Extremely complex decision trees better suited for custom apps
Clients who want bots but refuse to integrate with existing systems

If this problem sounds familiar, let's talk.
